title: Passive voice: be + past participle
explanation:
Passive voice (A2)

Subject + am/is/are + past participle (present passive)

Subject + was/were + past participle (past passive)

  • English is spoken all over the world.
  • The window was broken yesterday.

We use passive when the action is more important than who does it.

questions.text: English [*is spoken|is speaking|speaks] in many countries.
The letters [*were delivered|delivered|were delivering] this morning.
The museum [*is visited|visits|is visiting] by thousands of tourists.
My phone [*was stolen|stole|was stealing] on the bus.
Rice [*is grown|is growing|grows] in many parts of Asia.

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The homework ____ by every student. | *was done | did | was doing
Spanish ____ in Argentina. | *is spoken | speaks | is speaking
The bridge ____ last year. | *was built | built | was building
These cars ____ in Germany. | *are made | make | are making
The film [*was directed|directed|was directing] by a famous director.
This room [*isn't used|doesn't use|isn't using] very often.

questions.feedback: Passive question: Was + subject + past participle.
